title: Ming Foundation RFC Dissent and Unresolved Objection Register

items.majority_position: Affected-person or domain review is required before Candidate for standards governing people.
items.minority_position: Candidate may permit bounded deferral when incompleteness is explicit.
items.risk_if_ignored: Either premature authority or unsafe ambiguity.
items.required_resolution: Define review classes and safe deferral criteria.
items.day11_disposition: Partially addressed by ADR-0013 affected-person review gate; remains open pending actual review.
items.round2_disposition: Majority gate retained; minority bounded-deferral position remains preserved and requires explicit Accepted deferral criteria.

items.topic: Direct child participation versus representative control
items.majority_position: Participation should increase with capacity and impact.
items.minority_position: Direct participation can create safety and legal risks.
items.risk_if_ignored: Either premature authority or unsafe ambiguity.
items.required_resolution: Create jurisdiction- and safeguarding-qualified participation profiles.
items.day11_disposition: Addressed in RFC-0001 participation and conflict rules; remains open pending affected-person and jurisdiction review.
items.round2_disposition: Needs further participation and authority profiles plus affected-person review.
items.day13_disposition: PROF-0001 and PROF-0002 provide a proposed combined architecture; dissent remains Open pending affected-person and jurisdiction review.

items.majority_position: Material high-impact interpretations should be contestable.
items.minority_position: Some safety judgments require temporary non-disclosure.
items.risk_if_ignored: Either premature authority or unsafe ambiguity.
items.required_resolution: Define delay, expiry, review, and appeal.
items.day11_disposition: Addressed through bounded temporary restriction and later-notice rules; remains open pending safety review.
items.round2_disposition: Combined architecture accepted internally; temporary restriction, later notice, expiry, and appeal remain subject to safety review.

items.majority_position: A governed shared layer prevents shadow profiles.
items.minority_position: Centralization increases breach and surveillance risk.
items.risk_if_ignored: Either premature authority or unsafe ambiguity.
items.required_resolution: Compare centralized, federated, and hybrid models.
items.day11_disposition: Addressed by permitting centralized, federated, or hybrid authority maps; remains open pending architecture review.
items.round2_disposition: Combined centralized, federated, or hybrid authority-map position accepted internally; external architecture review remains.

items.majority_position: High-impact safety handling requires accountable human or service ownership.
items.minority_position: Non-staffed tools should limit scope rather than promise handoff.
items.risk_if_ignored: Either premature authority or unsafe ambiguity.
items.required_resolution: Define service profiles including no-handoff systems.
items.day11_disposition: Addressed by explicit active-handoff, bounded-review, no-handoff, and unverified service profiles; remains open pending operational review.
items.round2_disposition: Combined service-profile position accepted internally; operational evidence remains required.
items.day13_disposition: PROF-0004 provides evidence-backed service classes without forcing false handoff promises; dissent remains Open pending operational review.

items.majority_position: Least-intrusive reasonable action should be default.
items.minority_position: Severe uncertainty may justify more intrusive temporary action.
items.risk_if_ignored: Either premature authority or unsafe ambiguity.
items.day11_disposition: Addressed through a proportionality and precautionary-basis record; remains open pending safety review.
items.round2_disposition: Combined least-intrusive and precautionary temporary-action position accepted internally; safety review remains.
items.day13_disposition: PROF-0004 preserves transparent no-handoff behavior while RFC-0003 preserves proportionality; dissent remains Open pending safety review.

items.majority_position: Risk-banded thresholds are more appropriate than one number.
items.minority_position: Fixed minimums are easier to audit.
items.risk_if_ignored: Either premature authority or unsafe ambiguity.
items.required_resolution: Define conservative default and exception review.
items.day11_disposition: R2 item; no Day 11 source change.
items.round2_disposition: Outside Day 12 scope; remains open for RFC-0004 R2.

items.majority_position: Material boundaries must be visible before sensitive use.
items.risk_if_ignored: Either premature authority or unsafe ambiguity.
items.day11_disposition: R2 item; no Day 11 source change.
items.round2_disposition: Outside Day 12 scope; remains open for RFC-0005 R2.
